home.hero.lead: I help engineering leaders fix brittle engineering systems by strengthening architecture, sharpening technical decisions, and improving how teams communicate under pressure.

home.expertise.heading: Three levers of Progressive Engineering
home.expertise.1.title: Architecture That Holds Up
home.expertise.1.description: Brittle systems make every change feel risky. I redesign boundaries, dependencies, and responsibilities so the codebase can absorb change instead of resisting it.
home.expertise.2.title: Communication That Clarifies Decisions
home.expertise.2.description: Slow delivery is often a communication problem disguised as a technical one. I make ownership, trade-offs, and escalation paths explicit so teams stop losing time in ambiguity.
home.expertise.3.title: AI Leverage With Judgment
home.expertise.3.description: AI is valuable when the engineering system can support it. I use agents to accelerate analysis and implementation, but the differentiator is still human judgment, review discipline, and code that is structured enough to change safely.
home.philosophy.heading: What Progressive Engineering means
home.philosophy.p1: Progressive Engineering is not a slogan about novelty. It is the disciplined work of making engineering systems less brittle, decisions clearer, and change safer over time.
home.philosophy.p2: For engineering leaders, the outcome is simple: higher speed without gambling on production. That requires architecture that holds up, communication that reduces ambiguity, and technical leadership willing to name the real constraint.
home.philosophy.p3: AI belongs in that picture, but only as leverage. When the system is vague, brittle, or politically unclear, AI amplifies the mess. When the system is coherent, it compounds the team's effectiveness.
home.philosophy.quote: Safer change at higher speed is not a tooling problem. It is the result of better architecture, clearer decisions, and teams that communicate honestly.
home.cta.heading: If delivery is slow because the engineering system is brittle, we should talk.
home.cta.lead: I work with engineering leaders who need safer change, clearer technical decisions, and less friction between architecture and execution.
work.img.alt: Profile picture of Martin Trenker, independent software consultant
work.title: Martin Trenker
work.subtitle: Independent Software Consultant
work.about.heading: About me
work.about.p1: Across 19 engagements, from global enterprises to smaller product teams, the pattern was consistent: when architecture is brittle and technical decisions stay implicit, delivery slows down regardless of stack.
work.about.p2: That is the work I call Progressive Engineering. I help teams strengthen architecture, improve technical decision quality, and communicate clearly enough to move faster without increasing risk.
work.contact.heading: Contact
work.skills.heading: Skills
work.skills.programming.heading: Programming
work.skills.org.heading: Organizational
work.section.cleanAgile.heading: Architecture
work.section.cleanAgile.p: Architecture is where change either gets easier or more expensive. I work on boundaries, coupling, and system shape so teams can move faster without recurring structural drag.
work.section.learner.heading: Communication
work.section.learner.p: Most expensive technical problems are half-technical and half-communicative. I make ownership, constraints, and trade-offs explicit so teams can decide earlier and escalate less.
work.section.automation.heading: AI Leverage
work.section.automation.p: AI becomes useful when a team can steer it with judgment. I use agents as force multipliers for well-structured work and help teams build the habits that keep speed from turning into noise.

work.spotlight.heading: Selected Work
